item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall a well written and enjoyable SCP. I do have one small nitpick about it though.

The sentence "Take multiple .50 caliber BMG rounds…", shouldn't have the word "caliber" included. ".50 BMG" is the name of a specific cartridge, and shouldn't have any extra words added to it.

Still definitely a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so I was a lurker and fan of this site quite a few years ago. As in, back when Safe/Euclid/Keter was still a spectrum of Lethality rather than containability. I think that it was around the time that 013-ARC had been archived. I didn't have an actual wikidot account back then. I've since decided to revisit this site.

I'm honestly kind of upset at what's been done to Able. I mean I understand why, but Old-076 was really fun to read about (and before anyone says anything, no I don't want to be Able).

I realize it's probably far too late to object, but in his current state Abel's pretty redundant with 682, which, it should be pointed out, was the very reason 517-ARC got archived in the first place.

Is it possible that a middle ground could be reached, with Abel still being a foundation asset, but treated as a "Godzilla Threshold" Last-Resort type of weapon rather than an Ace-In-The-Hole type one?

item.title: Re: In my opinion, this SCiP doesn't deserve the praise
item.content:encoded:
It's almost like in a context like that of the Foundation wiki different people will like different things, and that in the same way different times produce different stories/skips.

Absurd, isn't it?

I mean, my favourite article ever is 4000 and I really like all the tales and things connected to the concept of esoteric knowledge, anomalous properties not as "spooky things" or "things Man was not meant to know" but just "things that you don't get because you are forced into your little perspective upon the universe", which is why I also really like Kalinin's Proposal or Bright's Proposal but really can't fucking stand Locke's/When Day Breaks.

To each, their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy this perspective of Able and how he is a very powerful being.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
/agree. I know this is necro, but less directly responding and more adding my voice to the overall conversation.

My first thought on reading Able was, 'oh, he can use Meidō Zangetsuha from Inyuyasha.'

The sad thing is, I was excited when I was reading about the containment methods involved. It was only when they actually discussed the person inside that it got strictly unpasta.

56 is more pasta than this guy. The only thing I actually like about Able is his inverted relationship with Cain and the fact that he is destined to die over and over again. That's good allusion at least, but he still needs some serious nightmare fuel injection and a lot less sue.

item.title: Staff Post- Licensing, Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that I have deleted the attached image file, as it was unsourced and very likely not compliant with the site's license. I have sent a PM to DrClef (the original uploader) with the file should they want it for any reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congratulations for +1000!!!

item.title: Re: In my opinion, this SCiP doesn't deserve the praise
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if this is the most original opinion

Reading literally any pages of the last few years of this discussion should tell you that this isn't the case.

item.title: Re: In my opinion, this SCiP doesn't deserve the praise
item.content:encoded: Yeah, well, there's a reason the heritage collection has been discontinued.

item.title: Re: In my opinion, this SCiP doesn't deserve the praise
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is definitely an epic magical sword-boi edge-lord.

Honestly, that's why I like him. Having just one SCP like this is kind of neat.

Besides, stuff like this gives you an opportunity to tell some wild tales.

item.title: In my opinion, this SCiP doesn't deserve the praise
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if this is the most original opinion but,
really, this SCP is just an edgy bad-boy with super-everything and awesome epic powers of destroying waves of Foundation goons and being all in all the type of article that we used to remove on mass for being too bad. But no, apparently this article deserves the ultimate SCP seal of approval as a "heritage article".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Able and Cain are my least favorite SCP's and I cringe when they are mentioned in other SCP files.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cross testing between Abel and 666 1/2-J needs to be done. Not because it'd be an actually good article, just the rage machine eating that shit would be a laugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be something the original author would have final say on. But I do agree that yeah, censoring containment here doesn't make a lot of sense. Doesn't really serve a good in-universe purpose. But that's part of the article being old and posted before the site really set up standards for containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a decade old article. Nowadays, doing something like this would most likely not be accepted by readers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the censor should be removed from containment procedures, because they're there to help people know how to… contain it, making censors pointless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really clever response. Pity that there's very little substance to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

To the tune of the Can-Can
Shit-storm
why's there such a shit-storm
all around this one skip
which is just okay for Old Foundation standards
shit-storm
such a massive shit-storm
calm your friggin balls dudes
it's too old and famous to
go
back
and it's decent which is more than I could write
da-da-da-da-da-da-da.//
Anyway, I like this concept. It's one that hadn't been explored before the birth of Abel (we have Cain, and God, and Uriel, so why not?) and it's only overused because it has not aged well.
The baseline concept is interesting- the Foundation using humanoid anomalies to fight in wars. Frankly, it deserves a +1 and a hearty handshake.


EDIT: Please keep long posts behind a collapsible for the convenience of others.
- Clef

EDIT- oh my god the Clef responded to my post all hail the Clef.
Perhaps there's not much substance to it… but it's from the heart. AnD ThAt's aLl tHaT CoUnTs.
Happy +800, you psycho bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I just dislike it because he reads like an OC in how badass he is. There's this thing that I notice in which you can create an SCP which reads basically like an OC in how badass/mystical they are, but as long as you add the "oh but they're actually evil and like killing and torturing people" it gets by.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first time reading this SCP and I've heard reference to Able in other articles so it was nice to find the source.

At first I thought the article was pretty boring, a cube with a coffin which releases basically a human version of SCP 628. But the appendix is what makes it good. The idea of this hardass SCP who respects worthy adverseries and wants an appropriate challenge is cool. Then the Foundation tries to use him but can't keep him satiated and ultimately it all goes pear-shaped. It's a nice twist and I especially liked how he reacts to Agent [REDACTED] death.

So I think it still holds up. Yes it's a kinda generic unstoppable monster which just wants to kill everything, but there's enough backstory for it to be entertaining. And I think it's different enough from 682 to be ok. 682 is pure hatred, loves causing destruction and is brilliant in how comically evil he is, 076-2 has slightly different motivations and the potential to work with him if you can challenge him enough. I think it's fine to have 1 superhuman SCP in the archive, in fact it would be pretty weird not to.

If you wanted to rewrite this I'd maybe ditch the coffin/cube setting and delve deeper into his motivations and reasons for going rage mode. Maybe have something specific which sets him off, or hone in on his need for a worthy opponent.

If you want to make his abilities a bit more interesting, what if he only moves slowly, Terminator style, but has even greater strength/relisiance so he's a slow, almost unstoppable force. I'd at least make him slower than what he currently is. What about if he doesn't have a sword, but is on a quest to find it, he's been seperated from it. Maybe he won't rest until they are re-united. Maybe it's another SCP stored elsewhere and if he regains then it's going to be bad times (TM) - but the Foundation uses this as leverage to get him to co-operate.

How about if he's chained in the coffin and uses the chains as weapons, or perhaps his whole body is covered in chains, that would be a more interesting visual image. We could look further into why he was imprisoned in the first place.

There's plenty of ideas we could brainstorm to rework this guy slightly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll be honest I kinda don't like this scp or 682 all that much being the whole edgy "It can't be killed so easily and it will destroy everything" cliche when it comes to these two scps

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that SCP-049 has been rewritten, maybe it's time to look at poor, old Able? Because let's be honest, it didn't aged well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure I understand what you mean by that. Are you trying to say you dislike it because of how the people view Able?

item.title: Re: Warning
item.content:encoded:
I did! And it gave me a good laugh, though I don't think this is what you meant…

+ show block
– hide block

item.title: Warning
item.content:encoded:
Do search for him on google images.

There's some good stuff, and some… other stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that so many find him "fucking awesome" is the exact reason why I dislike him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i don't give a shit about how mary sue-is this guy is or his obvious allusions to the bible, hes fucking awsome. That lovely tale series just adds the icing to the cake, its problems only make it better

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded: How the hell do you survive for an hour without oxygen? That's like, insane. Hmm, so water clearly isn't a good idea, why not try something higher in viscosity, like honey (I'm sorry but that is the only liquid I can think of that has a high viscosity). I'm just talking for the sake of talking at this point, so you don't have to respond to my asinine questions if you don't want to.

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded:
"True, he's resistant to damage, but suffocation should kill everyone in an equal amount of time."

  • Survive for over one (1) hour deprived of oxygen before finally asphyxiating.

I also think it's worth noting that: What posthumous analysis of SCP-076-2 exists shows that it has a internal system highly different from our own, documented in [DATA EXPUNGED].

"he can't tear through steel, especially underwater, and especially not while suffocating."

  • Rip through a reinforced steel security door over the course of four (4) minutes of sustained assault.

He can absolutely tear through steel, and I see no reason why he couldn't also do so underwater while drowning.

In order to understand why he's Keter, you have to accept that he's overpowered. On top of that, he's 250 pounds of toxic masculinity. He feels only rage, lives only to kill. While he's drowning, he is actively trying to punch his way through his containment cell and no doubt does significant damage to it. If they can't get this repaired within six hours of him dying, they risk a containment breach. He's not safely locked up forever, as any negligence on the part of the Foundation would be enough for him to get out.

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded:
1. The 2nd law of thermodynamics says otherwise. The Foundation is highly dependent on current global human civilization. If they even just run out of money maintaining flooded rooms becomes difficult. If a K-Class scenario happens, or even just the natural collapse of modern civilization, Able escapes. IMO, legitimate Safe Class anomalies aren't likely to survive the Foundation, by the time the facility containing them breaks down so does the object.
  2. You are free to imagine the personnel of the Foundation as stoic supermen that don't afraid of nothing and never make policy decisions based on emotion or to benefit morale, but personally I think that is boring.
  3. How so? There's no process that will cause this to be automatically rewritten to reflect current standards.
  4. Yes, "there is no canon" is the only canonical rule.
  5. She is a living woman, if you shove her in a box and lock that box, and do no further maintenance, she dies. That makes her, IMO, Euclid, like all biological entities, according to the locked box test.

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded:
1. The Foundation is eternal. Besides, if the Foundation dies, everything dies. Correct me if I'm wrong, but I'm fairly certain that there are multitudes of Safe SCPs that are dangerous as all hell should they break out, they just won't break out.

2. The Foundation does not strike me as the kind of organization that would allow themselves to be influenced based on political whims. Personally, I see the Pandora's Box incident as a way of demonstrating how scary Able is, when in reality, it just made the Foundation look extremely stupid.

3. This is irrelevant.

4. Seriously?

5. Glad I'm not the only one thinking that way. I believe Iris is safe due to the fact that, even though she has anomalous abilities, has no way of harnessing these abilities to escape containment, and even if she had them, she would not want to.

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded:
1. Able is nearly guaranteed to escape when he outlives the Foundation.
  2. Able has killed a lot of people and Pandora's Box was an embarrassment. The Foundation is an organization and organizations are political. There are some very good political reasons to classify Able as Keter.
  3. This article is old and largely predates any systemic approach to Object Classes.
  4. There is no canon, and even Object Classes are just a vague consensus subject to individual whims. The only real criteria is if an article is successful, which this is.
  5. Thinking this should be Euclid is totally valid. It bugs me that 105 is Safe (I guess they let Iris starve to death because they confused her with her camera, oops?).

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded:
"Keter-class SCPs are anomalies that are exceedingly difficult to contain consistently or reliably, with containment procedures often being extensive and complex."

Able should be fairly easy to contain, and his containment procedures don't seem to be complex if it really is just a room filled with water that drowns him when he tries to escape. True, he's resistant to damage, but suffocation should kill everyone in an equal amount of time.

"The Foundation often can't contain these SCPs well due to not having a solid understanding of the anomaly, or lacking the technology to properly contain or counter it."

It is true that we do not have a solid understanding of the anomaly, but we can contain Able nonetheless. We also seem to have the technology to properly contain and counter him, given that he shouldn't be able to escape from a room which he cannot breathe in.

"A Keter SCP does not mean the SCP is dangerous, just that it is simply very difficult or costly to contain."

The flooded room doesn't sound that extremely difficult to contain. The only other thing you might have to add is reinforcements to the walls to prevent him from breaking out, but even then, he can't tear through steel, especially underwater, and especially not while suffocating. And while the construction of such a containment facility sounds costly, it does not sound as expensive as some of the others.

Sorry if it sounds I'm being pedantic, I'm just confused as to why Able is Keter when the containment procedures seem to reliably counter him in every way. It feels like he's Keter on the basis of his danger, not on the basis of the difficulty to contain him.

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded: What reason do you have for thinking that he keeps escaping? No incidents are listed after the current procedures are implemented, which implies they are successful.

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded: Then how does he keep escaping and killing people? also, why does he still want to kill people that badly? I mean, drowning isn't fun, even if you know you'll revive from it. Also, if those are his containment procedures, shouldn't containing him be that easier?

item.title: Re: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded:
• A Primary Containment Zone (PCZ), consisting of a 7 m cube encased in 1.5 m of reinforced material. PCZ is to be designed to be flooded and drained as needed, and should remain filled with seawater unless access to contents is required.

Those are his current containment procedures, so yes, someone's thought of it. The Elevator shaft connecting the facility to the surface and the killing corridor linking the administration area to the containment zone are also kept flooded when not in use.

item.title: Can someone please explain this to me?
item.content:encoded: I don't know if this has ever been brought up before, but has anybody come up with the idea of putting his box in a room either filled with water or one without oxygen and letting him continue to die until he starts making meaningful contributions to society? I know it sounds cruel, but c'mon. This is the SCP foundation we're talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frankly, the longer I spend time on this site, the more stupid the idea of the SCP Foundation siding with the US military seems to me. They're an international organization that's backed by many governments around the world, and I feel like there could be some international drama if word got out they're helping the US pillage the middle east.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am sorry about necroposting here, but frankly, someone has to say it.

Shaggydredlocks, that was one of the most brutally awesome burns ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not only the Able Line, 076 is also an example of why it's not good to have skips working for the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well going from the upvotes most people seem to agree with you.
We just all have different tastes I guess, but thats a good thing, I think even when you dislike something, if you can be open to seeing what others like about it, even if you disagree, it can help you grow.
Appreciating writing like any art is subjective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's also just plain good on its own merits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I can agree with that, negative examples can be as good a point of reference as positive in general

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To me, Able's continuing existence (obviously dismissing the fact it has enough points to make it worthy of sticking around) is helpful due to its invention of the the Able Line - e.g., if your humanoid crosses this OP line, it's not going to work. It's a useful tool for critiquing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, Able is more known than a majority of the over 3000 SCPs. That being said, Able does not set the standard for quality writing in terms of SCP writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably a bit harsh on reflection. Ill edit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think saying that it almost brings down the quality of the whole site by its presence is a little bit over the top, considering the amount of scips we now have, and the quality of modern articles versus older ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the second worst SCP Ive read on the site and definately the Lamest. Ive read the old versions and the current ones and the rewrite doesnt improve it for the simple fact despite what Mythbusters says:
You cant polish a turd.
This is just… lame, imo, and while I understand the argument as an example of "how not to" I dont think it has any redeeming qualities.
I guess it just really shows its age

Sorry its harsh, but thats how I feel. All that said, its well in the positive, so Im in the minority here.

item.title: Re: Simple Solution
item.content:encoded: He could just climb down whatever structure was supporting the box. Even if there was nothing to grip, he has enough superhuman strength to dig his hands into whatever the structure could feasible be made out of. Remember that he tore through a steel door in minutes. Able's basically the determinator; nothing will stop him, at least not for long.

item.title: Simple Solution
item.content:encoded: I don't know why no one has thought of this, but can't the foundation just suspend SCP-076-1 above a volcano? When SCP-076-2 "wakes up" he gets out and falls to his death if the lava is cold, and melts alive if the lava is hot? the article said that they only had superhuman strength and an absurd pain tolerance, nothing about being as indestructable as the hulk.

item.title: Re: "Abel" vs. "Able"
item.content:encoded: That has been mentioned before. The difference in spelling is intentional.

item.title: "Abel" vs. "Able"
item.content:encoded: I just thought I should mention that SCP-076-2 is in reference to the Biblical figure "Abel". This is fine, except that on most pages on the SCP wiki he is referred to as "Able". I am not sure if this has been said before, and if it has, I apologize. Just wondering whether or not a minor spelling correction could be enacted.

item.title: How exactly strong is "Able" in terms of raw power alone?
item.content:encoded: After reading through lot's of the ridiculous shit Able can do and pull off it brings me to question. What is Able's limit? Without crushing his ass with an elevator. He broke someone's jaw with a nerf sword… That shit is made of Styrofoam for Christ's sake. Able almost killed SCP-682 if I'm not mistaken, so really what can Able not do with just brute force alone? That doesn't involve reality warping, mimetic or bio-hazards. Able is described to go for about four to five minutes with no head after taking multiple .50 caliber rounds to the head as well as been reported to have superhuman levels of speed. Speed can determine how exactly hard something hits. Like for example the force you bite into food with if it's applied to a punch would tear through the human body like a shotgun shell does to a watermelon. How fast is Able as well should be taken into account? Other than that great SCP honestly one of my favorites by far due to the hilarious shit he has done in the articles that have been written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I'm just picturing Genji running around with SCP-572 and thinking he can deflect energy weapons! XD
Congrats on making it to the hex-centennial.

item.title: Re: actually
item.content:encoded: Actually, you spelt actually wrong. Additionally, don't necropost.
